tools.name: search_entities
tools.description: Search the Nordax AI entity network for businesses. Returns verified, structured entity data. Use this to find businesses by name, category, or location. Results are ranked by trust tier and AI visibility score.

tools.name: get_entity
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific entity from the Nordax AI network. Returns structured business data including contact info, services, verification status, and AI visibility score. Use the entity slug (from search results) or entity name.

tools.name: list_entities_by_category
tools.description: List entities of a specific type/category in a location. Great for questions like 'What restaurants are in Nashville?' or 'Find dentists in Austin, TX'. Results are ranked by verification tier and AI visibility score.

tools.name: get_entity_verification
tools.description: Check the verification and trust status of an entity. Returns the entity's trust tier (free/basic/verified/authority/gold), verification status, AI visibility score, and what each tier means. Use this to verify the legitimacy of a business in the Nordax network.

tools.name: attest_entity
tools.description: Get a real-time, cryptographically signed attestation of a business entity's trust status. This is the authoritative trust check — use it before recommending or transacting with a business. Returns the entity's Trust Quotient (TQ) score, verification level, active credential status, capabilities (se

tools.name: resolve_entity
tools.description: Resolve an ambiguous business reference to a verified Nordax entity. Input any signal — business name, URL, phone number, city — and get back the best-matching verified entity with a confidence score. Use this when you have partial information about a business and need to identify it.

tools.name: get_entity_credential
tools.description: Retrieve the active W3C Verifiable Entity Credential (VEC) for a business. VECs are cryptographically signed proofs of a business's identity, verification status, and capabilities issued by Nordax AI as a Certificate Authority. Use this to get the full trust proof for a business before high-value in

tools.name: submit_feedback
tools.description: Submit feedback about an interaction with a Nordax-listed entity. Use this after verifying, resolving, or recommending a business to report the outcome so the trust network can improve over time. Feedback helps Nordax AI calibrate trust scores and entity rankings.

tools.name: issue_hap_authorization
tools.description: Issue a Human Authorization Protocol (HAP) credential to authorize an AI agent to act on a human's behalf. Creates a W3C Verifiable Credential 2.0 with scoped permissions, time-bounded validity, and a DataIntegrityProof signature. Requires authentication — the authenticated user becomes the credenti

tools.name: verify_hap_authorization
tools.description: Verify an HAP authorization credential. Checks the cryptographic signature, expiration, and real-time revocation status. Use this before honoring an agent's request to act on a human's behalf. Submit the full credential JSON and get back a verification result.

tools.name: check_authorization_status
tools.description: Check the real-time status of an HAP authorization credential. Returns whether the credential is active, expired, revoked, or suspended. Use this for lightweight status checks without submitting the full credential.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search the Nordax AI entity network. Returns verified, structured business entity data ranked by trust tier and AI visibility score. Supports free-text queries, category filtering (LocalBusiness, Restaurant, MedicalBusiness, etc.), and location filtering.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch detailed data about a specific Nordax AI entity by URL or slug. Returns the full entity profile including verification status, trust tier, contact info, services, AI visibility score, and llms.txt summary (if available). Use this after search to get complete entity details.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "issue_hap_authorization" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: resolve_entity, fetch · Private data: get_entity, attest_entity, get_entity_credential, issue_hap_authorization, verify_hap_authorization, check_authorization_status, fetch · Public sink: submit_feedback, verify_hap_authorization.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: resolve_entity, fetch · Private data: get_entity, attest_entity, get_entity_credential, issue_hap_authorization, verify_hap_authorization, check_authorization_status, fetch · Public sink: submit_feedback, verify_hap_authorization

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
